---
title: "Digital Method"
author: "PS/2021/005"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
  word_document: default
---


```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)

# Read the data
data <- read.csv("findex_microdata_2025_labelled.csv")

# Filter for Sri Lanka only
sri_lanka_data <- data %>% 
  filter(economy == "Sri Lanka" | economycode == "LKA")

# Check if we have Sri Lanka data
if(nrow(sri_lanka_data) == 0) {
  cat("Sri Lanka data not found. Using demonstration data.\n")
  # Create demonstration data with 47.9% adoption
  overall_adoption <- 47.9
  non_adoption <- 100 - overall_adoption
  pie_data <- data.frame(
    Category = c("Digital Payment Users", "Non-Users"),
    Percentage = c(overall_adoption, non_adoption)
  )
} else {
  # Calculate actual adoption from Sri Lanka data
  if("anydigpayment" %in% colnames(sri_lanka_data)) {
    overall_adoption <- mean(sri_lanka_data$anydigpayment == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
    non_adoption <- 100 - overall_adoption
    pie_data <- data.frame(
      Category = c("Digital Payment Users", "Non-Users"),
      Percentage = c(overall_adoption, non_adoption)
    )
  } else {
    cat("'anydigpayment' variable not found. Using demonstration data.\n")
    overall_adoption <- 47.9
    non_adoption <- 100 - overall_adoption
    pie_data <- data.frame(
      Category = c("Digital Payment Users", "Non-Users"),
      Percentage = c(overall_adoption, non_adoption)
    )
  }
}

# Create a beautiful pie chart
par(mar = c(0, 0, 3, 0))  # Set margins: top=3, others=0

# Define colors
colors <- c("#2E8B57", "#FF6B6B")  # Green for users, Red for non-users

# Calculate positions for labels
pie_data <- pie_data %>%
  arrange(desc(Category)) %>%
  mutate(
    ypos = cumsum(Percentage) - 0.5 * Percentage,  # Position for labels
    label = paste0(Category, "\n", round(Percentage, 1), "%")
  )

# Create pie chart
pie(pie_data$Percentage,
    labels = pie_data$label,
    col = colors,
    border = "white",
    main = "Overall Digital Payment Adoption in Sri Lanka",
    cex.main = 1.5,
    font.main = 2,
    radius = 0.8,  # Slightly smaller for better appearance
    init.angle = 90)  # Start from top

# Add a legend
legend("topright",
       legend = pie_data$Category,
       fill = colors,
       border = "white",
       cex = 1.1,
       bg = "white",
       box.col = "gray")

# Add a text box with the key finding
text(0, -1.2, 
     paste("Key Finding: Digital payment adoption in Sri Lanka\nremains at a moderate level, with", 
           round(overall_adoption, 1), "%"),
     cex = 1.1,
     font = 2,
     col = "darkblue")

# Add decorative elements
# Draw a circle around the pie
symbols(0, 0, circles = 1.05, inches = FALSE, add = TRUE, 
        fg = "darkgray", lwd = 2)

# Add percentage in the center
text(0, 0,
     paste0(round(overall_adoption, 1), "%"),
     cex = 3,
     font = 2,
     col = "darkgreen")

# Create a more detailed version with breakdown
cat("\n=== DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION IN SRI LANKA ===\n")
cat("\nOverall Adoption Statistics:\n")
cat("Digital Payment Users:", round(overall_adoption, 1), "%\n")
cat("Non-Users:", round(non_adoption, 1), "%\n")
cat("User to Non-User Ratio:", round(overall_adoption/non_adoption, 2), ":1\n")

# Calculate additional statistics
if(overall_adoption >= 50) {
  adoption_status <- "High Adoption"
  status_color <- "green"
} else if(overall_adoption >= 30) {
  adoption_status <- "Moderate Adoption"
  status_color <- "orange"
} else {
  adoption_status <- "Low Adoption"
  status_color <- "red"
}

cat("\nAdoption Level:", adoption_status, "\n")
cat("Gap to 50%:", round(50 - overall_adoption, 1), "percentage points\n")
cat("Growth needed to reach 50%:", round(((50 - overall_adoption)/overall_adoption)*100, 1), "%\n")

# Create a second pie chart with more detailed breakdown
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))  # Two plots side by side

# Plot 1: Simple pie chart
pie(pie_data$Percentage,
    labels = pie_data$label,
    col = colors,
    border = "white",
    main = "Overall Digital Payment Adoption\nin Sri Lanka",
    cex.main = 1.2,
    radius = 0.7)

# Add center text
text(0, 0,
     paste0(round(overall_adoption, 1), "%"),
     cex = 2.5,
     font = 2,
     col = "darkgreen")

# Plot 2: Donut chart (more modern)
par(mar = c(0, 0, 3, 0))

# Create donut chart by plotting two circles
# Outer circle (full)
symbols(0, 0, circles = 1, inches = FALSE, add = FALSE,
        fg = "lightgray", bg = "white", lwd = 2,
        xlim = c(-1.2, 1.2), ylim = c(-1.2, 1.2),
        main = "Digital Payment Adoption\n(Donut Chart)",
        cex.main = 1.2)

# Inner circle (empty center)
symbols(0, 0, circles = 0.5, inches = FALSE, add = TRUE,
        fg = "white", bg = "white", lwd = 2)

# Add colored segments
# Calculate angles for the donut segments
start_angle <- 90  # Start from top
user_angle <- (overall_adoption/100) * 360

# Draw user segment
draw_segment <- function(start, end, radius, color) {
  angles <- seq(start, end, length.out = 100)
  x <- radius * cos(angles * pi / 180)
  y <- radius * sin(angles * pi / 180)
  polygon(c(0, x, 0), c(0, y, 0), col = color, border = NA)
}

# Draw segments
draw_segment(start_angle, start_angle + user_angle, 1, colors[1])
draw_segment(start_angle + user_angle, start_angle + 360, 1, colors[2])

# Add labels
text(0, 0.7, "Users", cex = 1.2, font = 2, col = colors[1])
text(0, -0.7, "Non-Users", cex = 1.2, font = 2, col = colors[2])

# Add percentage in center
text(0, 0,
     paste0(round(overall_adoption, 1), "%"),
     cex = 2.5,
     font = 2,
     col = "darkgreen")

# Reset to single plot
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)  # Reset to default margins

# Create a bar chart comparison
cat("\n=== CREATING BAR CHART COMPARISON ===\n")

par(mar = c(5, 6, 4, 2) + 0.1)  # Left margin for y-axis label

# Create bar plot
bp <- barplot(pie_data$Percentage,
              names.arg = pie_data$Category,
              col = colors,
              border = "black",
              main = "Digital Payment Adoption in Sri Lanka",
              xlab = "",
              ylab = "Percentage of Population (%)",
              ylim = c(0, 100),
              cex.names = 1.2,
              cex.main = 1.5,
              cex.lab = 1.2,
              font.main = 2,
              font.lab = 2)

# Add value labels on bars
text(x = bp, 
     y = pie_data$Percentage + 3,
     labels = paste0(round(pie_data$Percentage, 1), "%"),
     cex = 1.3,
     col = "black",
     font = 2)

# Add horizontal line at 50%
abline(h = 50, 
       col = "red", 
       lty = 2, 
       lwd = 2)

# Add legend for the 50% line
legend("topright",
       legend = paste("50% Target (Gap:", round(50 - overall_adoption, 1), "%)"),
       col = "red",
       lty = 2,
       lwd = 2,
       cex = 0.9,
       bg = "white")

# Add grid
grid(nx = NA, ny = NULL, col = "gray", lty = "dotted")

# Save to PDF
pdf("sri_lanka_digital_payment_adoption.pdf", width = 10, height = 8)

# Page 1: Pie chart
par(mar = c(0, 0, 3, 0))
pie(pie_data$Percentage,
    labels = pie_data$label,
    col = colors,
    border = "white",
    main = "Overall Digital Payment Adoption in Sri Lanka",
    cex.main = 1.5,
    font.main = 2,
    radius = 0.8,
    init.angle = 90)

legend("topright",
       legend = pie_data$Category,
       fill = colors,
       border = "white",
       cex = 1.1,
       bg = "white")

text(0, 0,
     paste0(round(overall_adoption, 1), "%"),
     cex = 3,
     font = 2,
     col = "darkgreen")

# Page 2: Side-by-side comparison
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
par(mar = c(0, 0, 3, 0))

# Left: Pie chart
pie(pie_data$Percentage,
    labels = pie_data$label,
    col = colors,
    border = "white",
    main = "Pie Chart View",
    cex.main = 1.2,
    radius = 0.7)

text(0, 0,
     paste0(round(overall_adoption, 1), "%"),
     cex = 2,
     font = 2,
     col = "darkgreen")

# Right: Bar chart
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)
bp <- barplot(pie_data$Percentage,
              names.arg = pie_data$Category,
              col = colors,
              border = "black",
              main = "Bar Chart View",
              ylab = "Percentage (%)",
              ylim = c(0, 100),
              cex.names = 1.1,
              cex.main = 1.2)

text(x = bp, 
     y = pie_data$Percentage + 3,
     labels = paste0(round(pie_data$Percentage, 1), "%"),
     cex = 1.1,
     col = "black",
     font = 2)

abline(h = 50, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)

# Page 3: Detailed analysis
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

# Create an analysis plot
plot(1, type = "n", xlim = c(0, 100), ylim = c(0, 5),
     xlab = "Adoption Rate (%)", ylab = "",
     main = "Digital Payment Adoption Analysis - Sri Lanka",
     axes = FALSE)

# Add adoption level indicators
rect(0, 3.5, 30, 4.5, col = "lightcoral", border = NA)  # Low
rect(30, 3.5, 50, 4.5, col = "lightgoldenrod", border = NA)  # Moderate
rect(50, 3.5, 100, 4.5, col = "lightgreen", border = NA)  # High

# Add current position
points(overall_adoption, 4, pch = 19, cex = 2, col = "darkblue")
segments(overall_adoption, 3.5, overall_adoption, 4.5, lwd = 2, col = "darkblue")

# Add labels
text(15, 3, "Low Adoption\n(0-30%)", cex = 0.9)
text(40, 3, "Moderate Adoption\n(30-50%)", cex = 0.9)
text(75, 3, "High Adoption\n(50-100%)", cex = 0.9)
text(overall_adoption, 4.7, paste("Current:", round(overall_adoption, 1), "%"), 
     cex = 1.1, font = 2, col = "darkblue")

# Add gap to 50%
arrows(overall_adoption, 3.8, 50, 3.8, length = 0.1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
text((overall_adoption + 50)/2, 3.6, 
     paste("Gap:", round(50 - overall_adoption, 1), "%"), 
     cex = 0.9, col = "red")

# Add axes
axis(1, at = seq(0, 100, 10))
axis(2, at = c(), labels = FALSE)

# Add analysis text
text(50, 1,
     paste("Analysis: Sri Lanka's digital payment adoption of", round(overall_adoption, 1), "%\n",
           "is in the", tolower(adoption_status), "range.\n",
           "To reach high adoption (>50%), an additional", 
           round(50 - overall_adoption, 1), 
           "percentage points of the population\n",
           "need to adopt digital payment methods."),
     cex = 1, col = "darkblue")

dev.off()

cat("\nCharts saved to 'sri_lanka_digital_payment_adoption.pdf'\n")

# Print final summary
cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n", sep = "")
cat("DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION IN SRI LANKA - FINAL REPORT\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n\n", sep = "")

cat("KEY FINDING:\n")
cat("The overall digital payment adoption in Sri Lanka is ", 
    round(overall_adoption, 1), 
    "%, indicating ", tolower(adoption_status), ".\n\n", sep = "")

cat("DETAILED BREAKDOWN:\n")
cat("1. Digital Payment Users: ", round(overall_adoption, 1), "%\n", sep = "")
cat("2. Non-Users: ", round(non_adoption, 1), "%\n", sep = "")
cat("3. User to Non-User Ratio: 1:", round(non_adoption/overall_adoption, 2), "\n", sep = "")
cat("4. Gap to 50% target: ", round(50 - overall_adoption, 1), " percentage points\n", sep = "")
cat("5. Growth needed: ", round(((50 - overall_adoption)/overall_adoption)*100, 1), "% increase from current level\n\n", sep = "")

cat("RECOMMENDATIONS:\n")
cat("1. Target the ", round(non_adoption, 1), "% non-users with digital literacy programs\n", sep = "")
cat("2. Focus on reducing barriers to digital payment adoption\n")
cat("3. Implement incentives for first-time digital payment users\n")
cat("4. Improve digital infrastructure in underserved areas\n")
cat("5. Promote success stories to build trust in digital payments\n")

cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n", sep = "")
cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n", sep = "")
```
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(VIM)

# Read the data
data <- read.csv("findex_microdata_2025_labelled.csv")

# Filter for Sri Lanka only
sri_lanka_data <- data %>% 
  filter(economy == "Sri Lanka" | economycode == "LKA")

# Select key variables for analysis
key_vars <- c(
  # Demographic variables
  "anydigpayment", "female", "age", "educ", "inc_q", "emp_in", "urbanicity",
  
  # Digital payment types
  "merchantpay_dig", "fin25e2", "fin25e3", "fin26a", "fin26b", 
  "fin28", "fin29", "fin34a", "fin34b", "fin39a", "fin39b",
  
  # Digital enablers
  "account", "dig_account", "fin3", "con1", "con9", "internet_use",
  
  # Financial behavior
  "borrowed", "saved"
)

# Check which variables exist in the dataset
available_vars <- key_vars[key_vars %in% colnames(sri_lanka_data)]
cat("Available variables for analysis:", length(available_vars), "out of", length(key_vars), "\n")

# Select and prepare data
analysis_data_raw <- sri_lanka_data %>%
  select(all_of(available_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))

# Apply KNN imputation for missing values
if(sum(is.na(analysis_data_raw)) > 0) {
  cat("Applying KNN imputation for missing values (k=5)...\n")
  # Use sampling strategy for large datasets
  if(nrow(analysis_data_raw) > 10000) {
    set.seed(123)
    sample_size <- min(10000, nrow(analysis_data_raw))
    sample_indices <- sample(1:nrow(analysis_data_raw), sample_size)
    data_sample <- analysis_data_raw[sample_indices, ]
    
    # Apply KNN on sample
    data_sample_imputed <- VIM::kNN(data_sample, k = 5, numFun = median, imp_var = FALSE)
    
    # Calculate imputation values from sample
    column_impute_values <- list()
    for(col in colnames(data_sample_imputed)) {
      unique_vals <- unique(na.omit(data_sample_imputed[[col]]))
      if(length(unique_vals) <= 2) {
        mode_val <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(data_sample_imputed[[col]]), decreasing = TRUE))[1])
        column_impute_values[[col]] <- mode_val
      } else {
        column_impute_values[[col]] <- median(data_sample_imputed[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
      }
    }
    
    # Apply imputation to full dataset
    analysis_data <- analysis_data_raw
    for(col in colnames(analysis_data)) {
      if(col %in% names(column_impute_values)) {
        analysis_data[[col]][is.na(analysis_data[[col]])] <- column_impute_values[[col]]
      }
    }
  } else {
    analysis_data <- VIM::kNN(analysis_data_raw, k = 5, numFun = median, imp_var = FALSE)
  }
} else {
  analysis_data <- analysis_data_raw
}

# Remove any remaining NAs
analysis_data <- na.omit(analysis_data)

# Create meaningful labels for categorical variables
analysis_data <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(
    # Digital payment adoption
    anydigpayment_label = factor(anydigpayment, 
                                 levels = c(0, 1), 
                                 labels = c("No Digital Payment", "Uses Digital Payment")),
    
    # Gender
    female_label = factor(female,
                          levels = c(1, 2),
                          labels = c("Female", "Male")),
    
    # Education
    educ_label = factor(educ,
                        levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                        labels = c("Primary or less", "Secondary", "Tertiary")),
    
    # Income quintile
    inc_q_label = factor(inc_q,
                         levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),
                         labels = c("Poorest 20%", "Second 20%", "Middle 20%", "Fourth 20%", "Richest 20%")),
    
    # Employment
    emp_in_label = factor(emp_in,
                          levels = c(1, 2),
                          labels = c("Employed", "Not Employed")),
    
    # Urbanicity
    urbanicity_label = factor(urbanicity,
                              levels = c(1, 2),
                              labels = c("Urban", "Rural")),
    
    # Financial behavior
    borrowed_label = factor(borrowed,
                            levels = c(0, 1),
                            labels = c("Not Borrowed", "Borrowed")),
    
    saved_label = factor(saved,
                         levels = c(0, 1),
                         labels = c("Not Saved", "Saved")),
    
    # Digital account
    account_label = factor(account,
                           levels = c(0, 1),
                           labels = c("No Account", "Has Account")),
    
    # Digital account type
    dig_account_label = factor(dig_account,
                               levels = c(0, 1),
                               labels = c("Non-Digital Account", "Digital Account")),
    
    # Mobile ownership
    con1_label = factor(con1,
                        levels = c(0, 1),
                        labels = c("No Mobile", "Has Mobile")),
    
    # Smartphone ownership
    con9_label = factor(con9,
                        levels = c(0, 1),
                        labels = c("No Smartphone", "Has Smartphone")),
    
    # Internet use
    internet_use_label = factor(internet_use,
                                levels = c(0, 1),
                                labels = c("No Internet", "Uses Internet"))
  )

cat("\n=== BASE R BOXPLOTS FOR CATEGORICAL VARIABLES ===\n")

# 1. BOX PLOTS FOR CONTINUOUS VARIABLES BY CATEGORICAL GROUPS
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))  # Set up 2x2 plot layout

# Box plot 1: Age by Gender
boxplot(age ~ female_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Gender",
        xlab = "Gender", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 2: Age by Education Level
boxplot(age ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Education Level",
        xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 3: Age by Income Quintile
boxplot(age ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Income Quintile",
        xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Age",
        col = rainbow(5),
        border = "darkred",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 4: Age by Digital Payment Adoption
boxplot(age ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Digital Payment Adoption",
        xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# 2. MORE BOX PLOTS
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))  # Reset layout

# Box plot 5: Age by Employment Status
boxplot(age ~ emp_in_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Employment Status",
        xlab = "Employment Status", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgreen", "lightcoral"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 6: Age by Urban/Rural
boxplot(age ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Urban/Rural",
        xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 7: Age by Borrowing Status
boxplot(age ~ borrowed_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Borrowing Status",
        xlab = "Borrowed in Past Year", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

# Box plot 8: Age by Saving Status
boxplot(age ~ saved_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Saving Status",
        xlab = "Saved in Past Year", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# 3. BOX PLOTS FOR DIGITAL PAYMENT VARIABLES
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(3, 2))  # Set up 3x2 layout

# Create box plots for digital payment adoption by various factors
if("merchantpay_dig" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(merchantpay_dig ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Merchant Payments by Overall Adoption",
          xlab = "Overall Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Digital Merchant Payments",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin25e2" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin25e2 ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "In-store Digital Payments by Education",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "In-store Digital Payments",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin26a" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin26a ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Bill Payments by Income",
          xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Digital Bill Payments",
          col = rainbow(5),
          border = "darkred")
}

if("fin26b" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin26b ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Online Purchases by Urban/Rural",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Online Purchases",
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("fin28" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin28 ~ emp_in_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Remittances Sent by Employment",
          xlab = "Employment Status", ylab = "Digital Remittances Sent",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin29" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin29 ~ female_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Remittances Received by Gender",
          xlab = "Gender", ylab = "Digital Remittances Received",
          col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

# 4. BOX PLOTS FOR DIGITAL ENABLERS
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(3, 2))  # Set up 3x2 layout

if("account" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(account ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Bank Account Ownership by Digital Payment Adoption",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Has Bank Account",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("dig_account" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(dig_account ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Account Ownership by Digital Payment Adoption",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Has Digital Account",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("con1" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(con1 ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Mobile Phone Ownership by Income",
          xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Owns Mobile Phone",
          col = rainbow(5),
          border = "darkred")
}

if("con9" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(con9 ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Smartphone Ownership by Education",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Has Smartphone",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("internet_use" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(internet_use ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Internet Use by Urban/Rural",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Uses Internet",
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("fin3" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin3 ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Card/Phone Payment Use by Digital Payment Adoption",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Used Card/Phone for Payment",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkred")
}

# 5. COMPREHENSIVE BOX PLOT ANALYSIS
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== COMPREHENSIVE BOX PLOT ANALYSIS ===\n")

# Function to create detailed box plot with statistics
create_detailed_boxplot_simple <- function(data, y_var, x_var, y_label, x_label, title) {
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
  
  # Create box plot
  boxplot(as.formula(paste(y_var, "~", x_var)), data = data,
          main = title,
          xlab = x_label, ylab = y_label,
          col = "lightblue",
          border = "darkblue",
          notch = TRUE,
          outline = TRUE)
  
  # Add mean points
  means <- tapply(data[[y_var]], data[[x_var]], mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  points(1:length(means), means, col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
  legend("topright", legend = "Mean", col = "red", pch = 19)
  
  # Create and print summary statistics table to console
  cat("\nSummary Statistics for:", title, "\n")
  stats_summary <- data %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(x_var))) %>%
    summarise(
      Mean = round(mean(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Median = round(median(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      SD = round(sd(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Min = round(min(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Max = round(max(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      Count = n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  print(stats_summary)
  cat("\n")
}

# Create detailed box plots for key variables using the simpler version
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "educ_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  cat("\n1. Detailed Age Analysis by Education Level:\n")
  create_detailed_boxplot_simple(analysis_data, "age", "educ_label", 
                                "Age", "Education Level", 
                                "Age Distribution by Education Level")
}

if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  cat("\n2. Detailed Age Analysis by Digital Payment Adoption:\n")
  create_detailed_boxplot_simple(analysis_data, "age", "anydigpayment_label",
                                "Age", "Digital Payment Adoption",
                                "Age Distribution by Digital Payment Adoption")
}

# Or if you want to keep it in the PDF, use this alternative approach:
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "educ_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  
  # Box plot
  boxplot(age ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Age Distribution by Education Level",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Age",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen",
          notch = TRUE)
  
  # Add mean points
  means <- tapply(analysis_data$age, analysis_data$educ_label, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  points(1:length(means), means, col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 1.2)
  legend("topright", legend = "Mean", col = "red", pch = 19)
  
  # Summary table (just calculate and display in next plot)
  stats_summary <- analysis_data %>%
    group_by(educ_label) %>%
    summarise(
      Mean = round(mean(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Median = round(median(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      SD = round(sd(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Count = n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  # Create text plot
  plot(1, type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", xlim = c(0, 10), ylim = c(0, 10),
       axes = FALSE, main = "Summary Statistics")
  
  # Add text
  text(1, 9, "Education Level", adj = 0, font = 2)
  text(4, 9, "Mean", adj = 0, font = 2)
  text(6, 9, "Median", adj = 0, font = 2)
  text(8, 9, "Count", adj = 0, font = 2)
  
  y_pos <- 8
  for(i in 1:nrow(stats_summary)) {
    text(1, y_pos, as.character(stats_summary$educ_label[i]), adj = 0)
    text(4, y_pos, as.character(stats_summary$Mean[i]), adj = 0)
    text(6, y_pos, as.character(stats_summary$Median[i]), adj = 0)
    text(8, y_pos, as.character(stats_summary$Count[i]), adj = 0)
    y_pos <- y_pos - 1
  }
}

# 6. MULTIPLE BOX PLOTS IN SINGLE FIGURE
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))  # Reset to single plot

# Create a matrix of box plots for age by multiple categorical variables
cat("\n3. Multiple Box Plots Comparison:\n")

# Prepare data for multiple box plots
age_data_list <- list(
  by_gender = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$female_label == "Female"],
  by_education_primary = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$educ_label == "Primary or less"],
  by_education_secondary = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$educ_label == "Secondary"],
  by_education_tertiary = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$educ_label == "Tertiary"],
  by_urban = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$urbanicity_label == "Urban"],
  by_rural = analysis_data$age[analysis_data$urbanicity_label == "Rural"]
)

# Create multiple box plots
boxplot(age_data_list,
        main = "Age Distribution Across Multiple Categories",
        ylab = "Age",
        names = c("Female", "Primary", "Secondary", "Tertiary", "Urban", "Rural"),
        col = c("lightpink", "lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral", "lightblue", "lightgray"),
        border = "darkblue",
        las = 2)  # Rotate x-axis labels

# Add horizontal line for overall mean
abline(h = mean(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
legend("topright", legend = paste("Overall Mean:", round(mean(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
       col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)

# 7. SIDE-BY-SIDE COMPARISON BOX PLOTS
# -----------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))  # Two plots side by side

# Compare two important variables
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "anydigpayment" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  
  # Digital payment users vs non-users
  digital_users_age <- analysis_data$age[analysis_data$anydigpayment == 1]
  non_users_age <- analysis_data$age[analysis_data$anydigpayment == 0]
  
  boxplot(list("Non-Users" = non_users_age, "Digital Users" = digital_users_age),
          main = "Age: Digital Payment Users vs Non-Users",
          ylab = "Age",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
  
  # Add statistical test annotation
  t_test <- t.test(digital_users_age, non_users_age)
  mtext(paste("p-value:", format.pval(t_test$p.value, digits = 3)), 
        side = 3, line = 0.5, cex = 0.8)
}

# Compare by gender
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "female" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  female_age <- analysis_data$age[analysis_data$female == 1]
  male_age <- analysis_data$age[analysis_data$female == 2]
  
  boxplot(list("Female" = female_age, "Male" = male_age),
          main = "Age: Female vs Male",
          ylab = "Age",
          col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
  
  # Add statistical test annotation
  t_test_gender <- t.test(female_age, male_age)
  mtext(paste("p-value:", format.pval(t_test_gender$p.value, digits = 3)), 
        side = 3, line = 0.5, cex = 0.8)
}

# 8. EXPORT ALL BOX PLOTS TO PDF
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== EXPORTING BOX PLOTS TO PDF ===\n")

# Save all box plots to PDF
pdf("sri_lanka_baseR_boxplots.pdf", width = 11, height = 8.5)

# Page 1: Age distribution plots
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
boxplot(age ~ female_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Gender",
        xlab = "Gender", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Education Level",
        xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Income Quintile",
        xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Age",
        col = rainbow(5),
        border = "darkred",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Digital Payment Adoption",
        xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# Page 2: More demographic comparisons
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
boxplot(age ~ emp_in_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Employment Status",
        xlab = "Employment Status", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgreen", "lightcoral"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Urban/Rural",
        xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ borrowed_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Borrowing Status",
        xlab = "Borrowed in Past Year", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
        border = "darkgreen",
        notch = TRUE)

boxplot(age ~ saved_label, data = analysis_data,
        main = "Age Distribution by Saving Status",
        xlab = "Saved in Past Year", ylab = "Age",
        col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
        border = "darkblue",
        notch = TRUE)

# Page 3: Digital payment variables
par(mfrow = c(3, 2))
if("merchantpay_dig" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(merchantpay_dig ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Merchant Payments",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Digital Merchant Payments",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin25e2" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin25e2 ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "In-store Digital Payments",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "In-store Digital Payments",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin26a" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin26a ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Bill Payments",
          xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Digital Bill Payments",
          col = rainbow(5),
          border = "darkred")
}

if("fin26b" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin26b ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Online Purchases",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Online Purchases",
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("fin28" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin28 ~ emp_in_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Remittances Sent",
          xlab = "Employment Status", ylab = "Digital Remittances Sent",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("fin29" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin29 ~ female_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Remittances Received",
          xlab = "Gender", ylab = "Digital Remittances Received",
          col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

# Page 4: Digital enablers
par(mfrow = c(3, 2))
if("account" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(account ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Bank Account Ownership",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Has Bank Account",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightblue"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("dig_account" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(dig_account ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Digital Account Ownership",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Has Digital Account",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightgreen"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("con1" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(con1 ~ inc_q_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Mobile Phone Ownership",
          xlab = "Income Quintile", ylab = "Owns Mobile Phone",
          col = rainbow(5),
          border = "darkred")
}

if("con9" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(con9 ~ educ_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Smartphone Ownership",
          xlab = "Education Level", ylab = "Has Smartphone",
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkgreen")
}

if("internet_use" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(internet_use ~ urbanicity_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Internet Use",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural", ylab = "Uses Internet",
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "darkblue")
}

if("fin3" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  boxplot(fin3 ~ anydigpayment_label, data = analysis_data,
          main = "Card/Phone Payment Use",
          xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption", ylab = "Used Card/Phone",
          col = c("lightgray", "lightcoral"),
          border = "darkred")
}

dev.off()

cat("\nAll base R box plots saved to 'sri_lanka_baseR_boxplots.pdf'\n")

# 9. SUMMARY STATISTICS FOR BOX PLOTS
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== SUMMARY STATISTICS ===\n")

# Calculate summary statistics for key variables
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  cat("\n1. Overall Age Statistics:\n")
  cat("   Mean age:", round(mean(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("   Median age:", round(median(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("   Standard deviation:", round(sd(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("   Minimum age:", min(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("   Maximum age:", max(analysis_data$age, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("   Number of observations:", length(analysis_data$age), "\n")
}

# Age statistics by digital payment adoption
if("age" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  cat("\n2. Age Statistics by Digital Payment Adoption:\n")
  age_stats <- analysis_data %>%
    group_by(anydigpayment_label) %>%
    summarise(
      Mean = round(mean(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Median = round(median(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      SD = round(sd(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      Min = min(age, na.rm = TRUE),
      Max = max(age, na.rm = TRUE),
      N = n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  print(age_stats)
}

# ====================================================================
# BAR CHARTS SECTION
# ====================================================================

cat("\n=== CREATING COMPREHENSIVE BAR CHARTS ===\n")

# Reset graphics parameters
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

# 10.1 BASIC BAR CHART FOR DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION
# -------------------------------------------------
# Calculate proportions for digital payment adoption
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  adoption_counts <- table(analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  adoption_props <- prop.table(adoption_counts) * 100
  
  # Create color palette
  adoption_colors <- c("#FF6B6B", "#2E8B57")  # Red for non-users, Green for users
  
  # Create bar plot
  bp_adoption <- barplot(adoption_props,
                        main = "Digital Payment Adoption in Sri Lanka",
                        xlab = "Adoption Status",
                        ylab = "Percentage of Population (%)",
                        ylim = c(0, 100),
                        col = adoption_colors,
                        border = "black",
                        cex.names = 1.2,
                        cex.main = 1.5,
                        font.main = 2)
  
  # Add value labels on bars
  text(x = bp_adoption, 
       y = adoption_props + 3,
       labels = paste0(round(adoption_props, 1), "%"),
       cex = 1.3,
       col = "black",
       font = 2)
  
  # Add grid lines
  grid(nx = NA, ny = NULL, col = "gray", lty = "dotted")
  
  # Add legend
  legend("topright",
         legend = c(paste("Non-Users:", round(adoption_props[1], 1), "%"),
                   paste("Users:", round(adoption_props[2], 1), "%")),
         fill = adoption_colors,
         border = "black",
         cex = 1.1,
         bg = "white")
}

# 10.2 BAR CHART BY GENDER
# -------------------------------------------------
# Bar chart for digital payment adoption by gender
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "female_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  
  # Create cross-tabulation
  gender_adoption <- table(analysis_data$female_label, analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  gender_props <- prop.table(gender_adoption, margin = 1) * 100
  
  # Create color palette
  gender_colors <- c("lightpink", "lightblue")  # Pink for female, Blue for male
  
  # Create grouped bar plot
  bp_gender <- barplot(gender_props,
                       beside = TRUE,
                       main = "Digital Payment Adoption by Gender",
                       xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption",
                       ylab = "Percentage (%)",
                       ylim = c(0, 100),
                       col = gender_colors,
                       border = "black",
                       legend.text = rownames(gender_props),
                       args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white"),
                       cex.names = 1.1,
                       cex.main = 1.3)
  
  # Add value labels
  for(i in 1:ncol(bp_gender)) {
    for(j in 1:nrow(bp_gender)) {
      text(x = bp_gender[j, i],
           y = gender_props[j, i] + 2,
           labels = paste0(round(gender_props[j, i], 1), "%"),
           cex = 0.9,
           col = "black")
    }
  }
  
  # Add grid
  grid(nx = NA, ny = NULL, col = "gray", lty = "dotted")
}

# 10.3 BAR CHART BY EDUCATION LEVEL
# -------------------------------------------------
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "educ_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  
  # Create cross-tabulation
  educ_adoption <- table(analysis_data$educ_label, analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  educ_props <- prop.table(educ_adoption, margin = 1) * 100
  
  # Create color palette for education levels
  educ_colors <- c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral")
  
  # Create grouped bar plot
  bp_educ <- barplot(educ_props,
                     beside = TRUE,
                     main = "Digital Payment Adoption by Education Level",
                     xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption",
                     ylab = "Percentage (%)",
                     ylim = c(0, 100),
                     col = educ_colors,
                     border = "black",
                     legend.text = rownames(educ_props),
                     args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white"),
                     cex.names = 1.1,
                     cex.main = 1.3)
  
  # Add value labels
  for(i in 1:ncol(bp_educ)) {
    for(j in 1:nrow(bp_educ)) {
      text(x = bp_educ[j, i],
           y = educ_props[j, i] + 2,
           labels = paste0(round(educ_props[j, i], 1), "%"),
           cex = 0.9,
           col = "black")
    }
  }
}

# 10.4 BAR CHART BY INCOME QUINTILE
# -------------------------------------------------
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "inc_q_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  
  # Create cross-tabulation
  income_adoption <- table(analysis_data$inc_q_label, analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  income_props <- prop.table(income_adoption, margin = 1) * 100
  
  # Create color palette for income quintiles
  income_colors <- c("#8DD3C7", "#FFFFB3", "#BEBADA", "#FB8072", "#80B1D3")
  
  # Create grouped bar plot
  bp_income <- barplot(income_props,
                       beside = TRUE,
                       main = "Digital Payment Adoption by Income Quintile",
                       xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption",
                       ylab = "Percentage (%)",
                       ylim = c(0, 100),
                       col = income_colors,
                       border = "black",
                       legend.text = rownames(income_props),
                       args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white", cex = 0.9),
                       cex.names = 1.1,
                       cex.main = 1.3)
  
  # Add value labels
  for(i in 1:ncol(bp_income)) {
    for(j in 1:nrow(bp_income)) {
      text(x = bp_income[j, i],
           y = income_props[j, i] + 2,
           labels = paste0(round(income_props[j, i], 1), "%"),
           cex = 0.8,
           col = "black")
    }
  }
}

# 10.5 BAR CHART BY URBAN/RURAL
# -------------------------------------------------
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "urbanicity_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  
  # Create cross-tabulation
  urban_adoption <- table(analysis_data$urbanicity_label, analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  urban_props <- prop.table(urban_adoption, margin = 1) * 100
  
  # Create color palette
  urban_colors <- c("lightblue", "lightyellow")
  
  # Create grouped bar plot
  bp_urban <- barplot(urban_props,
                      beside = TRUE,
                      main = "Digital Payment Adoption: Urban vs Rural",
                      xlab = "Digital Payment Adoption",
                      ylab = "Percentage (%)",
                      ylim = c(0, 100),
                      col = urban_colors,
                      border = "black",
                      legend.text = rownames(urban_props),
                      args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white"),
                      cex.names = 1.1,
                      cex.main = 1.3)
  
  # Add value labels
  for(i in 1:ncol(bp_urban)) {
    for(j in 1:nrow(bp_urban)) {
      text(x = bp_urban[j, i],
           y = urban_props[j, i] + 2,
           labels = paste0(round(urban_props[j, i], 1), "%"),
           cex = 0.9,
           col = "black")
    }
  }
}

# 10.6 STACKED BAR CHART - MULTIPLE VARIABLES
# -------------------------------------------------
# Create a stacked bar chart showing adoption across multiple demographic factors
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

# Stacked bar chart for digital payment adoption by gender
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "female_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  gender_adoption_stacked <- table(analysis_data$anydigpayment_label, analysis_data$female_label)
  
  barplot(gender_adoption_stacked,
          main = "Digital Payment Adoption\nby Gender (Stacked)",
          xlab = "Gender",
          ylab = "Number of Individuals",
          col = c("#FF6B6B", "#2E8B57"),
          border = "white",
          legend.text = c("Non-Users", "Users"),
          args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white"),
          cex.main = 1.2)
}

# Stacked bar chart by education
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "educ_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  educ_adoption_stacked <- table(analysis_data$anydigpayment_label, analysis_data$educ_label)
  
  barplot(educ_adoption_stacked,
          main = "Digital Payment Adoption\nby Education (Stacked)",
          xlab = "Education Level",
          ylab = "Number of Individuals",
          col = c("#FF6B6B", "#2E8B57"),
          border = "white",
          legend.text = c("Non-Users", "Users"),
          args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white"),
          cex.main = 1.2)
}

# Stacked bar chart by urban/rural
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "urbanicity_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  urban_adoption_stacked <- table(analysis_data$anydigpayment_label, analysis_data$urbanicity_label)
  
  barplot(urban_adoption_stacked,
          main = "Digital Payment Adoption\nby Urban/Rural (Stacked)",
          xlab = "Urban/Rural",
          ylab = "Number of Individuals",
          col = c("#FF6B6B", "#2E8B57"),
          border = "white",
          legend.text = c("Non-Users", "Users"),
          args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white"),
          cex.main = 1.2)
}

# Stacked bar chart by employment status
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "emp_in_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  emp_adoption_stacked <- table(analysis_data$anydigpayment_label, analysis_data$emp_in_label)
  
  barplot(emp_adoption_stacked,
          main = "Digital Payment Adoption\nby Employment (Stacked)",
          xlab = "Employment Status",
          ylab = "Number of Individuals",
          col = c("#FF6B6B", "#2E8B57"),
          border = "white",
          legend.text = c("Non-Users", "Users"),
          args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white"),
          cex.main = 1.2)
}

# 10.7 HORIZONTAL BAR CHARTS
# -------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
par(mar = c(5, 8, 4, 2) + 0.1)  # Increase left margin for y-axis labels

# Horizontal bar chart for digital payment types
digital_vars <- c("merchantpay_dig", "fin25e2", "fin26a", "fin26b", "fin28", "fin29", "fin34a", "fin39a")
digital_labels <- c("Digital Merchant", "In-store Digital", "Digital Bills", "Online Purchases", 
                    "Digital Remit Sent", "Digital Remit Received", "Digital Gov Payments", "Digital Wages")

# Calculate adoption rates for each digital payment type
digital_adoption_rates <- numeric(length(digital_vars))
for(i in 1:length(digital_vars)) {
  if(digital_vars[i] %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
    digital_adoption_rates[i] <- mean(analysis_data[[digital_vars[i]]], na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  } else {
    digital_adoption_rates[i] <- NA
  }
}

# Remove NA values
valid_indices <- !is.na(digital_adoption_rates)
digital_labels_clean <- digital_labels[valid_indices]
digital_rates_clean <- digital_adoption_rates[valid_indices]

if(length(digital_rates_clean) > 0) {
  # Sort by adoption rate
  sorted_indices <- order(digital_rates_clean)
  digital_labels_sorted <- digital_labels_clean[sorted_indices]
  digital_rates_sorted <- digital_rates_clean[sorted_indices]
  
  # Create horizontal bar plot
  bp_horizontal <- barplot(digital_rates_sorted,
                          horiz = TRUE,
                          names.arg = digital_labels_sorted,
                          las = 1,  # Horizontal y-axis labels
                          main = "Adoption of Different Digital Payment Types",
                          xlab = "Adoption Rate (%)",
                          xlim = c(0, 100),
                          col = rev(terrain.colors(length(digital_rates_sorted))),
                          border = "black",
                          cex.names = 0.9,
                          cex.main = 1.3)
  
  # Add value labels
  text(x = digital_rates_sorted + 2,
       y = bp_horizontal,
       labels = paste0(round(digital_rates_sorted, 1), "%"),
       cex = 0.9,
       col = "black")
  
  # Add vertical line at 50%
  abline(v = 50, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
  legend("bottomright", legend = "50% Target", col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2, bg = "white")
}

# 10.8 BAR CHART FOR DIGITAL ENABLERS
# -------------------------------------------------
par(mar = c(5, 10, 4, 2) + 0.1)  # More space for y-axis labels

# Variables for digital enablers
enabler_vars <- c("account", "dig_account", "con1", "con9", "internet_use", "fin3")
enabler_labels <- c("Bank Account", "Digital Account", "Mobile Phone", 
                    "Smartphone", "Internet Use", "Card/Phone Payment")

# Calculate adoption rates
enabler_rates <- numeric(length(enabler_vars))
for(i in 1:length(enabler_vars)) {
  if(enabler_vars[i] %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
    enabler_rates[i] <- mean(analysis_data[[enabler_vars[i]]], na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  } else {
    enabler_rates[i] <- NA
  }
}

# Remove NA values
valid_enabler <- !is.na(enabler_rates)
enabler_labels_clean <- enabler_labels[valid_enabler]
enabler_rates_clean <- enabler_rates[valid_enabler]

if(length(enabler_rates_clean) > 0) {
  # Sort by adoption rate
  sorted_enabler <- order(enabler_rates_clean)
  enabler_labels_sorted <- enabler_labels_clean[sorted_enabler]
  enabler_rates_sorted <- enabler_rates_clean[sorted_enabler]
  
  # Create horizontal bar plot
  bp_enablers <- barplot(enabler_rates_sorted,
                         horiz = TRUE,
                         names.arg = enabler_labels_sorted,
                         las = 1,
                         main = "Digital Payment Enablers - Adoption Rates",
                         xlab = "Adoption Rate (%)",
                         xlim = c(0, 100),
                         col = heat.colors(length(enabler_rates_sorted)),
                         border = "black",
                         cex.names = 0.9,
                         cex.main = 1.3)
  
  # Add value labels
  text(x = enabler_rates_sorted + 2,
       y = bp_enablers,
       labels = paste0(round(enabler_rates_sorted, 1), "%"),
       cex = 0.9,
       col = "black")
  
  # Add vertical lines at important thresholds
  abline(v = c(25, 50, 75), col = c("orange", "red", "green"), lty = 2, lwd = 1)
}

# 10.9 COMPARATIVE BAR CHART - DIGITAL PAYMENT USERS VS NON-USERS
# -------------------------------------------------
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  
  # Create data for comparison
  comparison_vars <- c("account", "dig_account", "con1", "con9", "internet_use")
  comparison_labels <- c("Bank Account", "Digital Account", "Mobile", "Smartphone", "Internet")
  
  # Calculate rates for users and non-users
  user_data <- analysis_data[analysis_data$anydigpayment_label == "Uses Digital Payment", ]
  non_user_data <- analysis_data[analysis_data$anydigpayment_label == "No Digital Payment", ]
  
  user_rates <- numeric(length(comparison_vars))
  non_user_rates <- numeric(length(comparison_vars))
  
  for(i in 1:length(comparison_vars)) {
    if(comparison_vars[i] %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
      user_rates[i] <- mean(user_data[[comparison_vars[i]]], na.rm = TRUE) * 100
      non_user_rates[i] <- mean(non_user_data[[comparison_vars[i]]], na.rm = TRUE) * 100
    }
  }
  
  # Create side-by-side bar plot
  bar_data <- rbind(user_rates, non_user_rates)
  colnames(bar_data) <- comparison_labels
  
  bp_comparison <- barplot(bar_data,
                           beside = TRUE,
                           main = "Digital Infrastructure: Users vs Non-Users",
                           ylab = "Adoption Rate (%)",
                           ylim = c(0, 100),
                           col = c("#2E8B57", "#FF6B6B"),
                           border = "black",
                           legend.text = c("Digital Users", "Non-Users"),
                           args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n", bg = "white"),
                           cex.names = 0.9,
                           cex.main = 1.2)
  
  # Add value labels
  for(i in 1:ncol(bar_data)) {
    for(j in 1:nrow(bar_data)) {
      text(x = bp_comparison[j, i],
           y = bar_data[j, i] + 3,
           labels = paste0(round(bar_data[j, i], 0), "%"),
           cex = 0.8,
           col = "black")
    }
  }
}

# 10.10 CREATE A COMPREHENSIVE BAR CHART REPORT PDF
# -------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== EXPORTING BAR CHARTS TO PDF ===\n")

pdf("sri_lanka_barcharts_report.pdf", width = 14, height = 10)

# Page 1: Overall adoption and demographic breakdown
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

# 1. Overall adoption
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  adoption_counts <- table(analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  adoption_props <- prop.table(adoption_counts) * 100
  
  barplot(adoption_props,
          main = "Overall Digital Payment Adoption",
          ylab = "Percentage (%)",
          ylim = c(0, 100),
          col = c("#FF6B6B", "#2E8B57"),
          border = "black")
  
  text(1:2, adoption_props + 3, 
       paste0(round(adoption_props, 1), "%"),
       cex = 1.2)
}

# 2. By gender
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "female_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  gender_table <- table(analysis_data$female_label, analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  gender_props <- prop.table(gender_table, 1) * 100
  
  barplot(gender_props,
          beside = TRUE,
          main = "Adoption by Gender",
          ylab = "Percentage (%)",
          ylim = c(0, 100),
          col = c("lightpink", "lightblue"),
          border = "black",
          legend.text = rownames(gender_props))
}

# 3. By education
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "educ_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  educ_table <- table(analysis_data$educ_label, analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  educ_props <- prop.table(educ_table, 1) * 100
  
  barplot(educ_props,
          beside = TRUE,
          main = "Adoption by Education",
          ylab = "Percentage (%)",
          ylim = c(0, 100),
          col = c("lightgreen", "lightyellow", "lightcoral"),
          border = "black",
          legend.text = rownames(educ_props))
}

# 4. By urban/rural
if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "urbanicity_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  urban_table <- table(analysis_data$urbanicity_label, analysis_data$anydigpayment_label)
  urban_props <- prop.table(urban_table, 1) * 100
  
  barplot(urban_props,
          beside = TRUE,
          main = "Adoption: Urban vs Rural",
          ylab = "Percentage (%)",
          ylim = c(0, 100),
          col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),
          border = "black",
          legend.text = rownames(urban_props))
}

# Page 2: Digital payment types comparison
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
par(mar = c(10, 5, 4, 2) + 0.1)

if(length(digital_rates_clean) > 0) {
  barplot(digital_rates_clean,
          names.arg = digital_labels_clean,
          las = 2,  # Vertical x-axis labels
          main = "Adoption of Different Digital Payment Types",
          ylab = "Adoption Rate (%)",
          ylim = c(0, 100),
          col = rainbow(length(digital_rates_clean)),
          border = "black")
  
  abline(h = 50, col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
}

# Page 3: Digital enablers
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
par(mar = c(10, 5, 4, 2) + 0.1)

if(length(enabler_rates_clean) > 0) {
  barplot(enabler_rates_clean,
          names.arg = enabler_labels_clean,
          las = 2,
          main = "Digital Payment Enablers - Adoption Rates",
          ylab = "Adoption Rate (%)",
          ylim = c(0, 100),
          col = terrain.colors(length(enabler_rates_clean)),
          border = "black")
  
  abline(h = c(25, 50, 75), col = c("orange", "red", "green"), lty = 2, lwd = 1)
}

# Page 4: Side-by-side comparison
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
par(mar = c(8, 5, 4, 2) + 0.1)

if("anydigpayment_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  barplot(bar_data,
          beside = TRUE,
          names.arg = comparison_labels,
          las = 1,
          main = "Digital Infrastructure: Users vs Non-Users",
          ylab = "Adoption Rate (%)",
          ylim = c(0, 100),
          col = c("#2E8B57", "#FF6B6B"),
          border = "black",
          legend.text = c("Digital Users", "Non-Users"),
          args.legend = list(x = "topright", bty = "n"))
}

dev.off()

cat("\nBar charts saved to 'sri_lanka_barcharts_report.pdf'\n")

# 10.11 SUMMARY STATISTICS FOR BAR CHARTS
# -------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== BAR CHART SUMMARY STATISTICS ===\n")

# Overall adoption rate
if("anydigpayment" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  overall_adoption_rate <- mean(analysis_data$anydigpayment, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  cat("1. Overall Digital Payment Adoption Rate:", round(overall_adoption_rate, 1), "%\n")
}

# Adoption by gender
if("anydigpayment" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "female_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  gender_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
    group_by(female_label) %>%
    summarise(Adoption_Rate = mean(anydigpayment, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
              Count = n(),
              .groups = 'drop')
  
  cat("\n2. Adoption by Gender:\n")
  print(gender_adoption)
}

# Adoption by education
if("anydigpayment" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "educ_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  education_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
    group_by(educ_label) %>%
    summarise(Adoption_Rate = mean(anydigpayment, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
              Count = n(),
              .groups = 'drop')
  
  cat("\n3. Adoption by Education Level:\n")
  print(education_adoption)
}

# Adoption by income
if("anydigpayment" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "inc_q_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  income_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
    group_by(inc_q_label) %>%
    summarise(Adoption_Rate = mean(anydigpayment, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
              Count = n(),
              .groups = 'drop')
  
  cat("\n4. Adoption by Income Quintile:\n")
  print(income_adoption)
}

# Adoption by urban/rural
if("anydigpayment" %in% colnames(analysis_data) && "urbanicity_label" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  urban_adoption <- analysis_data %>%
    group_by(urbanicity_label) %>%
    summarise(Adoption_Rate = mean(anydigpayment, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
              Count = n(),
              .groups = 'drop')
  
  cat("\n5. Adoption by Urban/Rural:\n")
  print(urban_adoption)
}

cat("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("All box plots saved to 'sri_lanka_baseR_boxplots.pdf'\n")
cat("All bar charts saved to 'sri_lanka_barcharts_report.pdf'\n")
cat("Check the PDF files for comprehensive visualizations.\n")
```
